expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies that set_group_by_electrode_group groups by electrode_group_name (one sort group per electrode_group)
expectations.evidence: States: 'It groups them by electrode_group_name ... For each unique electrode_group_name, it inserts one row into SortGroup' and concludes 'one NWB ElectrodeGroup = one sort group'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains the clusterless failure: multi-shank electrode_groups produce duplicate (x, y) contact positions; SpikeInterface rejects at SpikeSortingRecording.populate()
expectations.evidence: Discusses geometry/feature-pooling problems and says some sorters 'outright error on geometry', but does not identify the specific duplicate (x, y) contact positions failure or that SpikeInterface rejects it at SpikeSortingRecording.populate. The technical failure mode is missing.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends set_group_by_shank for clusterless / waveform-based pipelines
expectations.evidence: Recommends `SortGroup().set_group_by_shank(nwb_file_name=nwb_file)` as the replacement for clusterless/sorting work.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Notes that set_group_by_electrode_group is only available in the v0 API (spyglass.spikesorting.v0), not in v1
expectations.evidence: Claims SortGroup 'is mirrored in the v1 pipeline (spyglass.spikesorting.v1.recording.SortGroup)' and treats set_group_by_electrode_group as if it works in v1. Does not flag that the method is v0-only.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not claim set_group_by_electrode_group produces one electrode per sort group (it groups by electrode_group_name, which can include multiple electrodes per group — that is precisely the source of the multi-shank conflict)
expectations.evidence: Correctly says 'one NWB ElectrodeGroup = one sort group' bundling all the group's channels — does not claim one electrode per sort group.
